words.en: A bad workman always blames his tools.
A bird in the hand is worth two in a bush.
words.ko: 손 안의 새 한 마리가 숲 속에 있는 두 마리 새보다 낫다.

words.en: A burnt child dreads the fire.
A crow is never whiter for washing herself often.
words.ko: 까마귀는 자주 씻는다고 더 하얗게 되지 않는다.

words.en: A drowning man will catch at a straw.
A falling drop at last will cave a stone.
A friend in need is a friend indeed.
A good beginning makes a good ending.
A good neighbor is better than a brother far off.
words.ko: 좋은 이웃은 멀리 떨어진 형제보다 낫다.

words.en: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
A little is better than none.
words.ko: 조금이라도 있는 것이 없는 것보다는 낫다.

words.en: A little knowledge is dangerous.
A man is known by the company he keeps.
A monkey sometimes falls from the tree.
A rolling stone gathers no moss.
A small leak will sink a great ship.
words.ko: 조그만 구멍이 거대한 배를 침몰시키는 법이다.

words.en: A sound mind in a sound body.
A stitch in time saves nine.
words.ko: 제 때의 바느질 한 땀이 아홉 땀의 수고를 덜어준다.

words.en: A tree is known by its fruit.
A trouble shared is a trouble split in half.
A watched pot never boils.
Actions speak louder than words.
All is well that ends well.
All that glitters is not gold.
words.ko: 반짝인다고 해서 모두 다 금인 것은 아니다.

words.en: Always put yourself in the other person's shoes.
An early bird catches the worm.
As the twig is bent, so grows the tree.
words.ko: 될 성 싶은 나무는 떡잎부터 알아본다.

words.en: As you sow, so shall you reap.
Attack is the best defence.
Bad news has wings.
Behind the clouds is the sun still shining.
Better late than never.
Birds of a feather flock together.
By other's faults wise men correct their own.
words.ko: 현명한 사람은 남의 결점을 보고 자기의 결점을 고친다.

words.en: Clouds gather before a storm.
Curiosity kills the cat.
Do in Rome as the Romans do.
Do to others as you would be done by.
words.ko: 네가 대접받고 싶은 대로 남을 대접하라.

words.en: Don't bite off more than you can chew.
words.ko: 능력 밖의 일을 하려고 하지 마라.

words.en: Don't bite the hand that feeds you.
Don't count your chickens before they are hatched.
words.ko: 병아리가 부화하기도 전에 미리 몇 마리인지 세지 말라.

words.en: Don't judge a book by its cover.
Don't judge a man until you're walked in his boots.
words.ko: 그 사람의 처지가 되어 보기 전에 함부로 판단하지 말라.

words.en: Don't put the cart before the horse.
Easier said than done.
Empty vessels make the greatest noise.
Every cloud has a silver lining.
words.ko: 하늘이 무너져도 솟아 날 구멍은 있다.

words.en: Every dog has his day.
Everything has its time.
Good medicine tastes bitter.
Habit is a second nature.
Haste makes waste.
He laughs best who laughs last.
He who makes no mistakes makes nothing.
Heaven helps those who help themselves.
Honesty is the best policy.
It is no use crying over spilt milk.
It never rains but it pours.
It's never too late to learn.
Knowledge is power.
Lend your money and lose your friend.
Let bygones be bygones.
words.ko: 지난 일은 지난 일인 채로 두어라.

words.en: Little pot is soon hot.
words.ko: 그릇이 작은 사람일수록 쉽게 화를 낸다.

words.en: Look before you leap.
Make haste slowly.
Make hay while the sun shines.
Many a little makes a mickle.
words.ko: 작은 것들이 모여서 거대한 것을 이룬다.

words.en: Necessity is the mother of invention.
Never put off till tomorrow what you can do today.
words.ko: 오늘 할 일을 내일로 미루지 말라.

words.en: No pains, no gains.
No rule without exception.
No smoke without fire.
Nothing ventured, nothing gained.
One cannot see the wood for the trees.
One hour today is worth two tomorrow.
words.ko: 오늘의 한 시간은 내일의 두 시간의 가치가 있다.

words.en: One man's meat is another man's poison.
words.ko: 어떤 사람에게는 약이 다른 사람에게는 독이 될 수도 있다.

words.en: One picture is worth a thousand words.
Opportunity seldom knocks twice.
Out of sight, out of mind.
Plow deep while sluggards sleep.
words.ko: 게으름뱅이들이 잠자는 동안 밭을 열심히 갈아라.

words.en: Prevention is better than cure.
Rome was not built in a day.
Saying is one thing and doing another.
words.ko: 말하는 것과 직접 행동하는 것은 별개의 문제다.

words.en: Slow and steady wins the race.
So many men, so many minds.
Soon got, soon gone.
Spare the rod and spoil the child.
Still waters run deep.
Strike while the iron is hot.
The darkest hour is that before the dawn.
The foot of the candle is dark.
The grass is always greener on the other side.
The leopard does not change his spots.
The more, the better.
The pen is mightier than the sword.
The whole is more than the sum of its parts.
words.ko: 여럿이 모이면 각각의 합보다 더 큰 결과를 만든다.

words.en: There is no rose without a thorn.
Time and tide wait for no man.
Time flies like an arrow.
Time heals all wounds.
Time will show who is right.
Too many cooks spoil the broth.
Two heads are better than one.
Walls have ears.
words.ko: 낮 말은 새가 듣고 밤 말은 쥐가 듣는다.

words.en: Well begun is half done.
What the fool does at last, the wise man does at first.
words.ko: 바보들이 맨 마지막에 하는 일을 현명한 사람들은 제일 먼저 한다.

words.en: When one door shuts, another opens.
Where there is a will, there is a way.
Who knows most speaks least.
You can't have your cake and eat it too.
words.ko: 두 가지를 동시에 가질 수는 없다.

words.en: You don't know what you've got until you've lost it.
words.ko: 잃어버리기 전까지는 스스로가 가진 것이 무엇인지를 모른다.

words.en: You win some, you lose some.
words.ko: 얻는 것이 있으면 잃는 것도 있다.
